name: Archon1
description: Robotically evolved archaerhodopsin variant with improved brightness, millisecond kinetics, and exceptional photostability for long-duration recordings.

spectrum.name: Archon1
spectrum.note: Emission: re-digitized from Song et al. 2024 (doi:10.1117/1.NPh.11.2.024201, Fig. 1b) — the first reported Archon1 emission spectrum (638 nm excitation, lambda-stack confocal, N=3 cells). In that measurement the emission peaks at ~670 nm; note this is bluer than the ~710 nm peak quoted for Archon in Monakhov et al. 2020 and Piatkevich et al. 2018, likely a consequence of the lambda-stack detection/filtering used by Song et al. A prior digitization of this same panel was mis-calibrated (peak stored at ~693 nm) and had a noisy, artifactual far-red tail crashing to ~0; the curve has been re-traced and overlay-verified against Fig. 1b (matches the corrected sibling Archon2 curve). Excitation: a Gaussian MODEL centered on Archon's absorption maximum (586 nm; measured for Archon2 by Penzkofer et al. 2021, doi:10.1016/j.jphotobiol.2021.112331). The only published Archon absorption (Penzkofer Fig. 1a) is a 15-curve photocycle-degradation series with the 'gLED 590 nm' reference spike drawn over the 586 nm peak and the inset/annotation covering the blue flank, so it cannot be cleanly digitized — the excitation curve is therefore modeled (shown dashed), not measured.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean τ of the bi-exponential fit F(t)=A(C·exp(-t/τfast)+(1-C)·exp(-t/τslow)), neuronal culture at 32 °C (n = 10 neurons). On: τfast 0.61 ms (88%) + τslow 8.1 ms (12%) → 1.51 ms. Off: τfast 1.1 ms (88%) + τslow 13 ms (12%) → 2.53 ms. Previously stored the fast components (0.61/1.1) alone; component amplitudes (C) are reported in Table S1.

Amplitude-weighted mean τ from biexponential fit in HEK293T cells at 30°C, n=6 cells (Ext. Fig. 4e). Activation: 87% fast τ 2.2 ms + 13% slow τ 9.1 ms → τ̄ = 0.87×2.2 + 0.13×9.1 = 3.10 ms. Deactivation: 86% fast τ 1.6 ms + 14% slow τ 9.2 ms → τ̄ = 0.86×1.6 + 0.14×9.2 = 2.66 ms. (Previously stored the fast component 2.2/1.6 alone; corrected to the weighted mean per policy. The paper's main text quotes only the fast components τ(on,fast) 2.2 ms / τ(off,fast) 1.6 ms.)

dynamicRangeData.note: Per 100 mV step in HEK293T cells, n=4 cells

sensitivityData.note: In-vivo ΔF/F per action potential = 22.4 ± 9.4% in mouse motor cortex layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons/interneurons (CAG / in utero electroporation), widefield 1P at 1.5 W/mm², 1 kHz (Suppl. Table 2). The per-100-mV in-vivo value in the same row is 23.5 ± 9.3% (this is the per-AP column, not the per-100-mV column).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM ≈ 2.5 ms (median of the Optical column of the Fig. 2h paired dot-plot; per-neuron means span ~1.8-3.3 ms, n=160 APs from 7 neurons, 5 cultures). Cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, widefield 1P (637 nm), image acquisition 2.3 kHz. The optical FWHM is broader than the paired electrical FWHM also plotted (~1.3-2.6 ms) — the difference reflects the sensor response kinetics plus the 2.3 kHz frame interval. Recording temperature not specified for this panel.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Independent optical single-AP half-width (FWHM) = 6.1 ± 0.3 ms (n = 9 neurons) measured in the Milosevic et al. 2020 eNeuro benchmarking study, cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, widefield 1P (568 ± 60 nm LED excitation / 605-30 nm exc filter / 665LP em), CCD acquisition at 500 fps (0.5 kHz). Substantially broader than the origin-paper Fig. 2h value (~2.5 ms at 2.3 kHz), reflecting the lower 500 Hz frame rate plus the sensor's slow-component kinetics. The paper also reports the optical AP was ~5.15× wider than the paired electrical AP half-width in the same trial. Recording temperature not stated for this cultured-neuron panel.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex layer-1 NDNF interneurons, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Cross-measured in the QuasAr6 paper (Tian et al. 2023): in-vivo optical single-spike FWHM of somArchon1 = 2.7 ± 0.5 ms (n = 23 cells, two animals), optogenetically triggered spikes in layer-1 NDNF cells of anesthetized mouse visual cortex, holographic structured-illumination 1P at a 1 kHz (996 Hz) frame rate. Co-measured against somQuasAr6a (2.4 ± 0.4 ms) and somQuasAr6b (2.3 ± 0.3 ms), reported statistically similar (NS, Wilcoxon rank-sum).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, hippocampal CA1 parvalbumin interneurons, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Cross-measured in the QuasAr6 paper (Tian et al. 2023): in-vivo optical single-spike FWHM of somArchon1 = 1.1 ± 0.15 ms (n = 24 cells, two animals), optogenetically triggered spikes in fast-spiking hippocampal CA1 parvalbumin interneurons of anesthetized mice, 1P at a 2 kHz frame rate. Co-measured against somQuasAr6b (0.87 ± 0.11 ms), which was narrower. The narrow spikes of PV neurons (patch FWHM < 0.5 ms) impose a stringent optical-detection challenge; also reported in Extended Data Fig. 8f.

brightnessData.note: Reciprocal of QuasAr6a 1.7x brighter than Archon1
Reciprocal of QuasAr6b 2.0x brighter than Archon1

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI photobleaching from the QuasAr6 paper Ext. Fig. 4f (Archon1-Citrine, cyan), HEK293T, continuous 635-nm laser at 420 W/cm² (= 4200 mW/mm²), 10 min, n=2 cells. The curve is bi-phasic (fast ~8% drop in the first 15 s, then a slow decline), so a single monoexponential fit was poor (r²≈0.81, τ≈1863 s); re-fit to a biexponential (a=0.058, τ1=3.0 s, τ2=2643 s; r²≈0.99). Archon1 is highly photostable here — it retains ~75% at the 585 s data edge (F=0.751), so the 0.75 crossing is only ~16 s past the last point: t75≈601 s, extrapolated:true (mild, deliberate). Similar to or slightly above QuasAr6a/6b, consistent with the paper's claim that QuasAr6 photostability is comparable to Archon1. Dose-scaled to the 100 mW/mm² 1P standard using the 635-nm red excitation intensity (4200 mW/mm²); this is the same Ext. Fig. 4f condition as QuasAr6a/6b, so the scaled ordering (Archon1 > QuasAr6a > QuasAr6b) is preserved. Primary subjects of this paper are QuasAr6a/6b.

Magenta Archon1 curve, 637 nm 800 mW/mm² (n=9 neurons). The MOST photostable of the five — retains ~93.5% at 900 s, so it never reaches 75% in-window (t75 omitted; that is the panel's headline result). Origin-paper photobleaching panel Fig. 2i, cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, continuous illumination. IMPORTANT: the five sensors in this panel were each imaged at a DIFFERENT intensity/wavelength (Ace2N-4aa-mNeon 13 mW/mm² 475 nm; QuasAr2 & Archer1 2200 mW/mm² 637 nm; Archon1 & Archon2 800 mW/mm² 637 nm), so the raw F/F0 cross-overlay (shared source+Fig. 2i) is NOT iso-condition — it reproduces exactly what the paper plotted. intensityMWmm2 = 800 records the 637-nm red excitation for dose-scaling, but this curve has no in-window t75 (never reaches 75% at 900 s), so it contributes no scaled value — the displayed t75 comes from the Ext. Fig. 4f 4200 mW/mm² entry.
